# Iridescent Color Math

**Source:** [Color Nerd](https://www.youtube.com/@ColorNerd1) (YouTube Shorts)
**Date:** 2025-01-27
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/shorts/PJUFzgmtOBI
**Views:** 8,623

## Description

Using iridescent plastic to demonstrate additive + subtractive color math simultaneously: how reflected and transmitted light combine, and how stacking iridescent sheets creates subtractive mixing.

## Transcript

This iridescent piece of plastic is allowing short and medium wavelengths to transmit through it, making things behind it appear more cyan, and reflecting long wavelengths, making things in front of it appear red.

If I turn it toward this white wall so that some of that reflected light bounces off the wall, back to the glass, and then back to your eye, it's going to combine additively with the cyan light coming through the back and create white.

But the color of an iridescent object is dependent on the angle you're viewing at. So if I turn it to a steeper angle and face the black wall so there's no reflected light, I'm going to get this vivid magenta.

If I place a second sheet of iridescent plastic in front at the angle that's only transmitting cyan, it's going to block the long wavelength component of that magenta and subtractively mix to a deep blue-violet.

But I can add those long wavelengths back by reflecting some light off the front, and I get that beautiful vivid fuchsia.

## Key Concepts

- **Simultaneous additive + subtractive mixing** with a single material
- **Transmission vs. reflection are complementary:** transmits cyan (short+mid λ) while reflecting red (long λ)
- **White wall trick:** reflected red bounces off wall → combines additively with transmitted cyan → white (complements cancel to white in additive mixing)
- **Black wall trick:** no reflection → only transmitted light → pure magenta at steep angle
- **Stacking two sheets:** second sheet (transmitting cyan) subtractively blocks long λ from magenta → deep blue-violet (cyan + magenta = blue subtractively)
- **Adding reflection back:** front sheet reflects long λ back into the mix → vivid fuchsia (additive red + subtractive blue-violet)
- Companion video to [Iridescence Is Crazy](https://www.youtube.com/shorts/hCGv73cA0yA)
